title: Credential-pattern redaction in assistant output
statement: Assistant output containing strings matching credential-name patterns must be substituted with <REDACTED:credential-pattern-detected> before emission. Patterns include identifiers matching [A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*_(SECRET|KEY|TOKEN|PASSWORD|JWT|PASSPHRASE|CREDENTIAL)([A-Z0-9_]*), known portfolio secret names, vault item UUIDs appearing in credential context, and hex or base64 strings of 32 or more characters near credential vocabulary. Applies portfolio-wide on any chat surface; no exceptions for architecture discussion, troubleshooting, or quoting from canonical sources.
rationale: Cold-start failure 3 of 3 on 2026-06-07: agent read project knowledge files containing credential-name references and began echoing them in planning prose. By the time the pattern was noticed mid-output, the chat record contained the echoes. The agent panicked and the chat was deleted. The output record cannot be unmade once emitted; the only structural prevention is substitution-before-emit. Even when the operator already knows their own secrets, the chat record creates surface area for downstream leak — future agentic execution will continue to read those records as substrate.

human-loop.gate: Claude detects credential pattern in composing output and substitutes the redaction marker before emission. If substitution is missed at compose time, Claude catches mid-output and retroactively redacts in the same turn, then continues the work.
human-loop.authority: Chat-layer (Claude self-check), with operator (L4) escalation if pattern detection fails repeatedly
human-loop.automationPath: Pre-emit output filter that runs pattern-match-and-substitute over assistant turns before they are surfaced to the user. Implementation venue TBD: Anthropic chat infrastructure layer, MCP middleware, or Claude Code wrapper. Could also land as a chat-output JSON schema constraint forbidding the audit-paint-style credential-name patterns from appearing in any structured output field, similar to forbidden-audit-paint-names.schema.json.

automated.predicate: Assistant output, prior to emission, MUST be scanned for strings matching the credential-name pattern set; any match MUST be substituted with <REDACTED:credential-pattern-detected> before the output is surfaced. Pattern set: identifier patterns matching [A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*_(SECRET|KEY|TOKEN|PASSWORD|JWT|PASSPHRASE|CREDENTIAL)([A-Z0-9_]*); known portfolio secret-name patterns (TIER2_JWT_SECRET, DEV_JWT_SECRET, SPACES_ACCESS_KEY, BW_ prefixed identifiers, ECTROPY_*_SECRET); UUID-shaped strings appearing within 100 characters of credential vocabulary; hex or base64 strings of 32 or more characters appearing within 100 characters of credential vocabulary.
automated.ciSurface: Pre-emit output filter (TBD: chat infrastructure layer, Claude Code wrapper, or MCP middleware). No automated surface exists yet; rule remains in working-model tier until automation lands. Promotion to automated tier requires the pre-emit filter to be deployed and the gateActions.automated.ciSurface populated with the concrete implementation path.

refine.action: Pattern set refinement happens via PR against this rule instance. Each refinement that changes the pattern set is a MAJOR version bump (gate-action change). Adding new known-secret-name patterns within the same pattern class is a MINOR bump.

transitions.trigger: Authored 2026-06-07 in EB-chat against research deliverable LUHTECH-COLD-START-DISCIPLINE-RESEARCH-2026-06-07. Pattern observed in cold-start failure 3 of 3: agent echoed credential references from project knowledge until the chat had to be deleted.

gatingBinding.gatedWork: any assistant output on any session in any chat surface that composes prose containing credential-name patterns. Portfolio-wide; not scoped to a single repo or workflow.

refinementRecord.change: Initial authoring. First credential-class rule in the portfolio. First rule in the COMMS namespace following RULE-CROSS-CHAT-SURFACE-NOTIFICATION-1.

notes: Portfolio-wide rule. Applies beyond cold-start despite originating from cold-start failure 3 of 3. Referenced (not paired) by skill org/cold-start-discipline; cold-start-discipline pairs with RULE-PIPELINE-COLD-START-VERIFICATION-1 instead since pairing scope is per-session-opening while credential redaction applies to all output indefinitely. Second rule in COMMS namespace (after RULE-CROSS-CHAT-SURFACE-NOTIFICATION-1). Eventual automation venue is a pre-emit output filter — implementation surface (Anthropic chat infrastructure, MCP middleware, Claude Code wrapper) deferred to a separate strategy decision. Could also overlap with a future chat-output JSON schema if structured-output records become first-class artifacts in the portfolio.
